items.title: LIC CFO Sunil Agrawal resigns months after his term was extended to March 2027
items.summary: Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) informed the stock exchanges of a change in its senior management, with Chief Financial Officer (CFO) Sunil Agrawal resigning after more than four years in the role.&nbsp;

items.title: Govt proposes 7 kg grain per beneficiary under Antyodaya Anna Yojana; family quota capped at 35 kg
items.summary: Draft National Food Security (Amendment) Bill aims to shift to a per-person entitlement of 7 kg, while maintaining the 35 kg household cap.

items.title: CSM Technologies IPO Day 2: GMP, subscription status, price band, review, other key details. Should you subscribe?
items.summary: CSM Technologies IPO price band has been set at  ₹107 to  ₹113 per share. At the upper-end of the IPO price band, the company aims to raise  ₹145.78 crore from the book-building issue, which is entirely a fresh issue of 1.29 crore equity shares.

items.title: Multibagger stock HFCL share price hits 5% upper circuit following broader gains on Dalal Street; up 200% in 3 months
items.summary: HFCL has been among the market's standout performers in recent months. The stock touched a 52-week high of  ₹220.10 on June 22, 2026, after recovering sharply from its 52-week low of  ₹59.83 recorded in January 2026.

items.title: FarEye finds place in Gartner’s Market Guide for Multicarrier Parcel Management
items.summary: Company ranks 11th overall as AI-powered platform gains recognition amid rising parcel delivery challenges

items.title: Swiggy faces leadership churn as Instamart COO Ankit Jain, CBO Hari Kumar resign; shares fall
items.summary: Swiggy is facing new leadership churn in its quick commerce business at a time when competition in the sector is accelerating. According to reports, Instamart Chief Operating Officer Ankit Jain and Chief Business Officer Hari Kumar have resigned from their roles.

items.title: Bodhi Tree Multimedia bags Assam government mandate to build state OTT platform
items.summary: Bodhi Tree Multimedia will spearhead Assam's official digital content platform, a significant move into platform ownership and recurring revenue. This initiative will showcase regional entertainment, culture, and heritage, accessible globally. The project, built with Mogi OTT on Google Cloud, signifies a strategic shift for Bodhi Tree, positioning them at the forefront of digital public infrastructure and cultural digitisation, potentially serving as a model for other states.

items.title: Stainless steel sector MSMEs flag import surge, urges govt to reintroduce quality control order
items.summary: Indian stainless steel MSMEs are urging the government to reinstate a quality control order after its suspension led to a surge in cheap Chinese imports. Industry bodies report a 65% year-on-year jump in imports, threatening domestic jobs and investments. Manufacturers claim these imports enter at predatory prices, undermining local businesses committed to quality and 'Make in India' principles.

items.title: Dalmia Bharat share price gains as Goldman Sachs upgrades to ‘Buy’; sees 17% upside
items.summary: Dalmia Bharat share price rose nearly 2% after Goldman Sachs upgraded it to a Buy. The target price was revised to  ₹2,020, indicating a 17% upside. Concerns exist over energy costs, demand outlook, and capacity additions, but easing tensions in the West Asia conflict support a positive view.

items.title: Brazil plans up to $735 million panda bond issuance, says finance minister
items.summary: Brazil is set to launch its inaugural panda bonds, aiming to raise up to 5 billion yuan ($735 million). This significant debut marks Brazil as the fifth sovereign issuer in China's domestic debt market within a year. Finance Minister Dario Durigan stated the move is a crucial "test" to facilitate Brazilian companies' expansion in China and offers a hedge against currency volatility for their investments.

items.title: India bonds jump ahead of auction as oil erases war-led gains
items.summary: Indian government bonds saw a significant rally for the fourth consecutive day, buoyed by falling oil prices and positive sentiment from foreign inflows and the Reserve Bank of India's cautious stance on rate hikes. This surge occurred ahead of a substantial debt sale, with benchmark yields dropping. Overseas investors are showing strong interest, marking the highest monthly inflow in two years, potentially driven by index inclusion expectations.

items.title: Areion Assets Management launches $60 million AIF to tap India's special situations market
items.summary: Areion Assets Management has launched a $60 million Category III AIF from GIFT City targeting India’s special situations market. The fund will invest in distressed assets, structured credit and IBC opportunities, focusing on recovery-led value creation across sectors, amid increasing institutionalisation of alternative investments in India.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Live: Sensex rises 500 pts, Nifty above 24,170 as Crude slips close to pre-war levels; IndiGo, Maruti lead gains
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices LIVE: Sensex gained 545.31 points, or 0.71 per cent, to 77,536.53, while the Nifty 50 rose 158.05 points, or 0.66 per cent, to 24,179.70 at 10.37 am

items.title: CSM Technologies IPO Day 2: subscribed 0.31 times so far, QIBs yet to bid
items.summary: The company is seeking to raise ₹145.78 crore through the IPO and has fixed a price band of ₹107-113 per share

items.title: Buy the dip, stay invested: Matt Orton sees more upside for global markets
items.summary: Global markets are gaining traction as geopolitical worries ease and AI companies report strong earnings. Matt Orton of Raymond James highlights that receding oil prices and sustained AI optimism are turning market dips into buying chances. He emphasizes that while the US dollar's strength poses challenges for emerging markets like India, focusing on quality companies and diversified investments remains key for continued gains.

items.title: Micron's AI Moment: Record earnings, strong guidance & what's next
items.summary: Micron reported a record quarter with strong revenue, profits and margins, driven by sustained demand for AI memory products. Strong guidance reinforced investor confidence, lifting semiconductor stocks globally. The company’s growing role in AI infrastructure and long-term agreements support expectations of stronger and less cyclical earnings growth.

items.title: SBI shares rise nearly 2% after analyst meet; how much more can PSU bank stock rally?
items.summary: State Bank of India (SBI) shares rose nearly 2 per cent on June 25 after the country's largest public sector lender continued to report strong business growth and healthy asset quality.

items.title: Rupee rises 31 paise to 94.24 against U.S. dollar in early trade
items.summary: A weaker greenback and positive sentiments in the domestic equity market further supported the local unit while heavy FII outflows prevented a sharper rise, forex traders said

items.title: Tata Chemicals shares rise 4% on hopes of Tata Sons listing after RBI’s new norms
items.summary: Tata Sons faces renewed scrutiny after RBI tightened rules for upper-layer NBFCs, reinforcing pressure for mandatory listings above Rs 1 lakh crore in assets. While the conglomerate exceeds the threshold, its de-registration request remains pending. The listing debate has also exposed divisions within Tata Trusts, with leadership opposing a listing while some trustees publicly support it, highlighting internal discord.

items.title: Govt tells public sector banks to expand deeper into Rural India
items.summary: The finance ministry wants public sector banks to open branches and improve banking access to villages that have a population of more than 3,000 people.

items.title: US Stock Market: Bessent applauds Fed communication reset, questions dot plot's relevance
items.summary: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has backed Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh's push to revamp the Fed's communication strategy, including a review of its widely watched dot plot interest-rate forecasts and forward guidance. Bessent argued that markets have become overly reliant on these projections, which often fail to accurately predict future policy moves.

items.title: Vedanta, NALCO, Hindustan Zinc shares fall up to 3% as silver, aluminium, other metal prices tumble. Here's why
items.summary: Metal stocks including Vedanta, NALCO and Hindustan Zinc fell up to 3% as global metal prices declined sharply. A stronger US dollar, easing Middle East tensions and rising expectations of a Fed rate hike pressured commodities like silver, aluminium, copper and zinc, triggering a broad-based pullback in the metal pack.

items.title: Rajesh Exports shares hit lower circuit as MCA orders SFIO probe amid mounting regulatory scrutiny
items.summary: The SFIO has been directed to complete the investigation and submit its report within the next six months. The probe is expected to examine the company's corporate governance practices, export transactions and other financial dealings.

items.title: Stock markets rally in early trade tracking drop in oil prices, positive trends in Asian equities
items.summary: The 30-share BSE Sensex climbed 440.23 points to 77,435.76 in early deals. The 50-share NSE Nifty went up by 137.80 points to 24,147.60

items.title: Sterlite Technologies surges 5% on QIP launch, sets Rs 613.69 floor price
items.summary: Sterlite Technologies' stock climbed nearly 5% to Rs 613.35 as it officially launched its Qualified Institutions Placement (QIP) to bolster its capital. The company's board and shareholders approved the fundraising, with the Authorization and Allotment Committee setting a floor price of Rs 613.69 per share. This move, coupled with positive technical indicators, has boosted investor confidence, driving the stock's significant rally.

items.title: LIC CFO Sunil Agrawal resigns, last day on July 14
items.summary: Resignation effective from close of business hours of July 14; State-owned insurer appreciates his valuable contributions and wishes him best

items.title: Indian airlines race to restore West Asia flights. But caution remains.
items.summary: IndiGo and Air India Express lead recovery in one of carriers’ key overseas markets, but overall capacity remains below last year as disruptions linger.

items.title: Civil Aviation Minister inaugurates first hub-and-spoke flight from Varanasi via Delhi
items.summary: Civil Aviation Minister K Ram Mohan Naidu launched Air India's 'Easy Connect' initiative in Varanasi, establishing the first hub-and-spoke flight to connect the city globally via New Delhi. This model aims to boost international connectivity from smaller cities. Six destinations will soon adopt this model, with Varanasi set to receive airport upgrades within 13 months. Air India plans to expand this service to several other Indian cities.

items.title: South Korean AI chip stock SK Hynix share price surges 13% after unveiling $29 billion US listing plan
items.summary: SK Hynix shares surged nearly 13% after announcing plans to raise $29.4 billion through a US listing, fueling a rally in South Korea's semiconductor sector. Investors anticipate strong AI demand, leading to significant stock price increases over various time frames.

items.title: India’s $55 billion green energy pipeline faces climate damage
items.summary: India's ambitious renewable energy expansion faces a significant climate threat, with $55 billion in planned solar, wind, and hydropower assets vulnerable to extreme weather by 2030. Ninety percent of proposed capacity across ten states is at high risk from events like floods and wildfires. This highlights the urgent need for resilience measures to protect investments and ensure the nation's clean energy transition remains on track.

items.title: Apple-Intel chip deal makes strategic sense but production is years away
items.summary: A deal, which neither company has formally announced, would pair Intel’s effort to rebuild its credibility as a contract chipmaker

items.title: Rupee rises 31 paise to 94.24 against US dollar in early
items.summary: In the interbank forex market, the rupee opened at 94.30 against the US dollar and strengthened to 94.24, up 31 paise from its previous close

items.title: Nifty Auto jumps over 2%; Maruti, TVS Motor, Ashok Leyland, M&M among top gainers. Why are auto stocks rising?
items.summary: On Thursday morning, major auto stocks, such as Maruti, TVS Motor, Ashok Leyland, Mahindra and Mahindra, Hero MotoCorp, and Eicher Motors, rose more than 2% each.

items.title: India-US trade deal enters crucial phase as Section 301 and tariff deadline loom large
items.summary: India and the US are in the final stages of trade deal negotiations, with a July 24 deadline looming. A key sticking point is Section 301 of the US Trade Act, which India fears could lead to the re-imposition of tariffs. Experts suggest a 10% chance of missing the deadline, which would revert talks to MFN tariffs and potentially stall market access discussions.

items.title: IndiGo, SpiceJet shares rally up to 4% as crude oil prices fall below pre-war levels after 42% crash
items.summary: Aviation stocks, including IndiGo and SpiceJet gained after crude oil prices declined sharply as supply concerns eased following progress in the Iran conflict. Lower fuel costs lifted sentiment for airlines, while investors assessed the potential recovery in travel demand and normalisation of global aviation operations.

items.title: Delhivery share sale: Logistics stock falls over 2% after Alpha Wave Ventures sells entire 1.93% stake
items.summary: Alpha Wave Ventures LP sold its entire 1.93% stake in Delhivery in two tranches on June 24. The Delhivery stake sale by Alpha Wave Ventures LP comes a few days after venture capital firm Nexus Venture Partners offloaded its 1.97% stake in Delhivery.

items.title: Vedanta Resources unit returns to dollar bond market to buy back high-cost notes, say bankers
items.summary: Vedanta Resources Finance II aims to raise ‌funds through six-, eight- and 11-year ‌bonds, ⁠with an initial coupon ⁠guidance of 7.25%, 7.6250% and 8%

items.title: Crude oil futures edge closer to pre-war levels
items.summary: At 10 am on Thursday, September Brent oil futures were at $72.89, down 1.33%, and August crude oil futures on WTI were at $69.36, down 1.39%

items.title: Avience Biomedicals share price lists at 90% premium over the IPO price
items.summary: Avience Biomedicals share price opened at  ₹395.20 apiece on NSE SME. The SME stock listed at 90% premium over the IPO price of  ₹208.

items.title: India's office market remains cautious despite resilient demand and selective rental gains: Report
items.summary: India's office market saw demand outpace new supply in Q2 FY26, leading to modest rent increases in active areas. Despite a cautious overall sentiment, the first half of 2026 recorded significant leasing activity, driven by Global Capability Centers and flexible workspace adoption. Bengaluru and Hyderabad led leasing, though Mumbai and Pune experienced a notable slowdown in large deals.

items.title: Rajesh Exports stock hits fresh lower circuit as ED alleges FEMA violations, record-keeping lapses
items.summary: The stock reaction follows the agency’s claims that the gold refiner and jewellery manufacturer failed to maintain critical transaction records and could not substantiate several overseas investments and trade settlements.

items.title: India-US trade deal near, but key issues still unresolved: Asia Society Policy Institute experts
items.summary: Trust deficit and tariff clarity remain major hurdles in finalising agreement

items.title: Insurgent consumer brands surpass $7.5 bn revenue in FY25, grow nearly 4x in five years: Report
items.summary: Insurgent brands are growing at 1.5 to 5 times their respective categories and are building new segments at breakneck speed, said the joint report by Bain & Company and DSG Consumer Partners

items.title: Stock Market Holiday 2026: Are NSE, BSE closed tomorrow on June 26 for Muharram? Check market closing dates list
items.summary: Indian stock markets will remain closed on June 26 for Muharram, marking another scheduled exchange holiday in 2026. Commodity exchanges will follow different trading schedules, with MCX resuming in the evening session while NCDEX remains shut. Several additional market holidays are scheduled later in the year.

items.title: Jewellery, defence, and banks: Nuvama's Nikhil Ranka reveals where to find real opportunities
items.summary: Nuvama Asset Management's Nikhil Ranka identifies key investment opportunities and potential pitfalls in India's equity markets. While retail faces valuation concerns, jewellery shines. FMCG stocks offer a potential catch-up trade. Defence presents a long-term growth story, and banking is poised for a swift recovery due to converging tailwinds. Textiles have largely seen their re-rating, urging selective investment.

items.title: Stock Market Today: Sensex jumps 400 points; Nifty above 24,100; IT and realty stocks shine
items.summary: Stock Market Today: At the opening bell, the BSE Sensex rose 400.72 points, or 0.52 per cent, to 77,391.94. The index opened at 77,391.07 against its previous close of 76,991.22.

items.title: Vikram Solar says NCLAT stays insolvency proceedings against company
items.summary: The appellate tribunal has put on hold the NCLT Kolkata order admitting CIRP against the company until the next hearing.

items.title: Vodafone Idea shares rally 80% in less than 3 months. Time to buy or avoid?
items.summary: Vodafone Idea shares have seen a dramatic surge, climbing nearly 80% in under three months. This rally, fueled by reduced dues and promoter support, has sparked investor interest. However, experts caution that while short-term gains are possible, underlying structural challenges persist, making it a trader's play rather than a long-term investment. Investors are advised to proceed with caution.

items.title: IRFC OFS opens for retail investors today. Should you participate?
items.summary: Non-retail investors fully subscribed to the IRFC OFS on Wednesday, placing bids for 1.86 times the shares reserved for their category. Following the strong response, the Centre has decided to exercise the greenshoe option.

items.title: US Stock Market: Retail investor rush sends Wendy's stock soaring on heavy trading volume
items.summary: Wendy's shares experienced a dramatic surge, climbing nearly 29% as retail investors flocked to the struggling fast-food chain. This rally, reminiscent of the 2021 meme stock craze, saw the stock trending on platforms like Stocktwits and Reddit.  The significant buying interest from individual traders comes as the company faces sluggish sales and activist pressure, with its stock having fallen substantially from its peak.

items.title: Sony Pictures invests $100 million in giant screen start-up Cosm
items.summary: Los Angeles-based Cosm is an operator of large-screen theatres that show films and sporting events

items.title: Broader market winners! How investors found 15 multibaggers in a muted Nifty year?
items.summary: Despite a subdued Nifty, several Indian stocks have surged, becoming multibaggers for investors. This outperformance is attributed to resilient corporate earnings, particularly in small and mid-cap segments, and company-specific growth drivers. While near-term challenges like rising costs and potential earnings downgrades exist, the overall FY27 outlook remains manageable, with easing geopolitical tensions expected to aid recovery. Investors are advised to focus on fundamentals over momentum.

items.title: Nifty reclaims 24,000 as crude slide and chip rally lift markets
items.summary: The Sensex opened at 77,391.07 and was trading at 77,335.31, up 344.09 points as of 9:20 AM; The Nifty 50 opened at 24,125.85 and was trading at 24,128.30, up 106.65 points

items.title: Missed the FII U-turn? 6 stocks turned multibagger after foreign investors corrected their mistake
items.summary: Foreign institutional investors (FIIs) reversed their selling trend in six select Indian stocks, turning them into multibaggers. Bajaj Consumer Care led the pack with a 265% surge after FIIs significantly increased their stake. Other beneficiaries include Acutaas Chemicals, SML Mahindra, Dee Development Engineers, United Foodbrands, and RateGain Travel Technologies, all witnessing substantial gains following FII re-entry. This shift highlights smart money's strategic course correction.

items.title: Gold languishes near 7-month low as Fed tightening bets boost dollar
items.summary: Spot gold was down 0.7% at $3,971.08 per ‌ounce, as of 0311 GMT. US gold futures for ‌August ⁠delivery lost 0.5% to $3,987

items.title: PM Modi reviews four major projects worth Rs 30,000 crore, calls for timely completion under PM Gati Shakti framework
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi chaired the 52nd PRAGATI meeting, reviewing infrastructure projects exceeding ₹30,000 crore to boost economic growth and connectivity. He stressed the PM Gati Shakti Master Plan for timely completion and discussed TB Mukt Bharat Abhiyan, leveraging digital tech. The PM also addressed concerns over cybercrime and digital arrests, urging coordinated action and faster grievance redressal to protect citizens.

items.title: Hub and Spoke flights to commence from 6 more cities in 6 weeks: Naidu
items.summary: Air India launches first Hub and Spoke service from Varanasi, which will connect to Delhi and from there to 9 international destinations

items.title: Sensex jumps 500 points, Nifty rises above 24,150 as oil prices cool below $73 per barrel
items.summary: Indian stock markets surged on Thursday, with Sensex and Nifty climbing significantly as falling oil prices bolstered investor confidence. Major gainers included IndiGo, M&M, Maruti Suzuki, and TCS, while broader markets also saw positive movement. The real estate and auto sectors led the gains, indicating a robust trading session for the day.

items.title: Gold prices fall Rs 6,000/10 gram in two days; silver tanks Rs 15,500/kg on rate hike fears. Time to sell precious metals?
items.summary: Gold and silver prices have fallen sharply on MCX over the past two sessions amid a stronger US dollar and rising rate hike expectations. Silver dropped Rs 15,500/kg while gold fell nearly Rs 6,000 per 10 grams. Analysts expect continued volatility and advise caution at current levels.

items.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Osho Krishan of Angel One suggests buying HDFC Bank, Emcure Pharma shares on 25 June
items.summary: Indian equity benchmarks opened higher today, driven by gains in Asian markets and easing crude oil prices. The Nifty 50 rose 0.43%, and market breadth remained positive with 15 out of 16 major indices in the green, showcasing investor confidence amid improving global sentiment.

items.title: Global Market: China's 'future industries' investment boom sparks valuation bubble concerns
items.summary: China is witnessing a massive surge in venture capital, pouring billions into futuristic sectors like space, quantum computing, and AI. This investment frenzy, driven by Beijing's ambition to lead in technology and reduce reliance on foreign tech, sees even revenue-less startups attracting significant funding. Investor confidence is high, with new listing rules easing domestic market access for these promising, government-backed ventures.

items.title: Monsoon and Markets: 7 sectors investors may avoid as El Nino looms
items.summary: Market expert Mehraboon Irani believes that pockets like agrochemicals, microfinance, two-wheelers, housing renovation, construction and select FMCG names warrant caution until more clarity emerges on monsoon.

items.title: Interglobe Aviation among 5 F&O stocks with a sharp rise in futures open interest
items.summary: Five NSE F&O stocks, including Oracle Financial Services, IndiGo, IRFC, Swiggy and Avenue Supermarts, saw sharp open interest build-up on June 24, signaling heightened trader participation and fresh futures positions.

items.title: US Stock Market: AI chip rally reignites as Micron, Qualcomm forecasts add over $400 billion in market value
items.summary: Chip stocks surged on Wednesday, adding over $400 billion in market value, fueled by optimistic forecasts from Micron Technology and Qualcomm.  Micron's strong outlook for memory chips and Qualcomm's ambitious data center growth targets reignited investor confidence in the AI trade. This positive sentiment boosted rivals and equipment suppliers, signaling a robust demand for AI-driven hardware and infrastructure.

items.title: 'There is no quick way to make money': Zerodha's Nithin Kamath issues warning that could save investors millions
items.summary: Zerodha co-founder Nithin Kamath highlights the persistence of pyramid schemes in India, with many investors misled by promises of easy profits. He cautions that this mindset can lead to significant financial losses, urging individuals to be wary of recruitment-based opportunities.

items.title: Bitcoin breaks below $60,000 again as biggest crypto buyers balk
items.summary: The cryptocurrency fell through a closely watched support level on Wednesday as investors grappled with growing concerns about Michael Saylor’s financing machine at Strategy Inc

items.title: CSM Technologies IPO Day 2 update: Subscribed 32%, GMP at 4%. Should you subscribe?
items.summary: CSM Technologies' IPO is seeing a subdued response on its second day, with only 32% subscription. Retail investors show more interest, subscribing to 53% of their quota. The grey market premium is a modest 4%, suggesting limited listing gains. The company, a digital transformation and GovTech solutions provider, aims to raise ₹145.78 crore. Analysts advise caution due to premium valuation and reliance on government contracts.

items.title: Gold rate falls on MCX on a stronger dollar amid rising expectations of US Fed rate hikes; what should investors do?
items.summary: Gold rate on the MCX declined in the morning on Thursday, 25 June, due to a stronger dollar. MCX gold August futures were 0.37% down at  ₹1,40,749 per 10 grams, while MCX silver July futures were 1.07% down at  ₹2,10,801 per kg around 9:10 am.

items.title: Rupee opens 37 paise higher at 94.30 against US dollar
items.summary: The rupee rose by 37 paise to 94.30 against the US dollar, driven by falling crude oil prices and likely RBI intervention, despite pressure from strong dollar demand and expectations of higher US interest rates.

items.title: IRFC shares fall 2% as OFS worth Rs 2,212 crore opens for retail investors today. Here's all you need to know
items.summary: Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) shares dipped as the government initiated an Offer for Sale (OFS), aiming to divest nearly 2% stake. The OFS, priced at a slight discount, saw strong institutional investor interest, leading to the exercise of the greenshoe option. Retail investors and employees can now participate in the sale, which aims to raise over Rs 2,212 crore.

items.title: Where is gold headed if the war ends?
items.summary: Gold, as a safe-haven investment, was clearly on investors' and traders' minds following the outbreak of war in West Asia. And sure enough, the price of gold initially went up, but then came the correction.

items.title: Forget power: The unquenchable AI thirst propelling water stocks up to 45%
items.summary: India's water sector is experiencing a surge, with stocks like Shakti Pumps and VA Tech Wabag jumping up to 45% in a month. This rally is fueled by increased government funding for water infrastructure and the growing demand from data centers for cooling. Experts predict wastewater treatment and recycling will be key growth areas, though current valuations reflect future optimism more than present earnings.

items.title: UltraTech or Dalmia Bharat: Which cement stock is better bet after recent correction?
items.summary: Cement stocks may recover after the second quarter despite near-term pressure from higher costs and seasonal weakness. The Nifty Cement index closed at 15,066.05 on June 24, up 249.45 points or 1.68 per cent.

items.summary: South Korea’s KOSPI surged as much as 6% to an intraday high of 8,982, extending gains for a second straight session and recovering sharply from earlier losses. Trading was briefly halted after a 5% jump triggered a sidecar mechanism. The rally follows a steep 10% drop earlier in the week driven by a selloff in domestic chip stocks.

items.summary: Amol Athawale from Kotak Securities identifies three stocks for the next 1-2 weeks, including PVR INOX and Aadhar Housing Finance, with strong technical indicators and favourable market conditions.&nbsp;

items.title: Bollywood bets on South heroines amid fading 'bankable' hindi stars
items.summary: In a notable trend, Bollywood producers are seeking established South Indian actresses to bridge the gap in reliable female leads. Casting directors point out the dwindling pool of dependable Hindi heroines and recognize the fresh energy that South Indian stars contribute. Popular across the nation from dubbed films, these actresses provide economic benefits and a welcomed variety in mainstream roles, fulfilling audience expectations for vibrant, new faces on screen.

items.summary: Indian stock market benchmark indices, Sensex and Nifty 50, are likely to open higher on Thursday, supported by a sharp decline in crude oil prices and broadly mixed global market cues. Traders will also track monthly expiry-related volatility and developments in global markets.

items.summary: The early-stage investor is looking to back 14-16 companies while increasing cheque sizes and expanding its focus on fintech, AI and sustainability.

items.summary: Silver has fallen as much as 14% this week, hitting a seven-month low as expectations of US Fed rate cuts fade and bond yields rise. Cooling geopolitical tensions and reduced safe-haven demand have added pressure. The metal is now trading at less than half of its January all-time high levels.

items.title: Advit Jewels IPO final bidding day: GMP hints at 38% listing gains. Should you subscribe?
items.summary: Advit Jewels IPO enters its final bidding day with strong investor interest, backed by robust subscription levels and a healthy grey market premium. items.title: RBI says no to special exposure relaxations for state NBFCs
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has firmly rejected requests for special exemptions from concentration norms for government-owned NBFCs, emphasizing a principles-based regulatory approach. The central bank also denied calls to raise single counterparty limits for public sector enterprises. While allowing grandfathering of residual breaches and specific conditions for temporary limit breaches, the RBI has tightened rules to ensure regulatory clarity and stability.

items.title: RBI tightens rules on recovery of excess pension payments
items.summary: Government pensioners will now have stronger protection against unexpected deductions from their accounts. The Reserve Bank of India has mandated that banks must inform pensioners and obtain their consent before recovering any excess pension payments. This new directive ensures that individuals are aware and agree to any adjustments, preventing abrupt debits and requiring a written undertaking from pensioners for future recoveries.

items.title: RBI eases forex exposure rules, giving banks more room to trade
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has updated foreign exchange exposure rules for banks.  These changes simplify calculations by merging onshore and offshore positions and allow inclusion of overseas earnings.  Crucially, banks can now exclude certain long-term foreign currency investments, potentially boosting trading capacity.  Forex risk capital will be based on actual net open positions, with gold treated separately.

items.title: Air India plane comes in front of IndiGo aircraft on same taxiway at Ahmedabad airport
items.summary: An Air India plane mistakenly turned onto the same taxiway as an IndiGo aircraft at Ahmedabad airport on Wednesday evening. The IndiGo flight, preparing for takeoff to Mumbai, was halted at a safe distance of approximately 200 meters from the Air India plane. Both airlines confirmed the incident, with IndiGo stating the aircraft came to a stop safely apart.

items.title: In the age of AI, telecom vendors say no device can be declared vulnerability-free
items.summary: Telecom equipment manufacturers are struggling with the government's demand for guaranteed vulnerability-free gear, citing the rapid evolution of cyber threats. Vendors met with the Department of Telecommunications seeking clearer policy guidelines for Pro Tem certificates, essential for selling products like 5G equipment. The government assured that National Centre for Communication Security is finalizing new policy guidelines soon, addressing industry concerns about self-declarations and potential penalties.

items.title: RBI in wait-and-watch mode, no certainty on rate hike: Governor Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated that an interest rate hike is premature, as inflation hasn't broadly impacted consumer prices. The central bank remains in a 'wait-and-watch' mode, closely monitoring global uncertainties and the monsoon's progress. While acknowledging improved outlooks, risks persist, but India is well-prepared for challenges. The RBI has revised inflation forecasts upwards and growth forecasts downwards for the fiscal year.

items.title: BSNL's corner office goes open market as Centre seeks new CMD
items.summary: The government is actively seeking a new Chairman and Managing Director for BSNL, opening the door to private sector professionals for the first time. Applicants must meet specific age and financial criteria, with a deadline of July 22. This strategic move aims to bring fresh leadership to the state-owned telecom giant, following an extension for the current interim chief.

items.title: India rewrites telecom rulebook, replacing licence contracts with statutory authorisations
items.summary: New government rules replace the telecom industry's contractual licensing with a unilateral statutory model. Operators will now receive authorization after meeting requirements, with future government directives becoming binding terms. Violations will be treated as statutory breaches, managed internally, and can be appealed to the TDSAT. This shift aims to streamline regulation, though telcos expressed concerns about investment uncertainty.

items.title: Delhi NCR's 'Ring of Opportunity' could open up a new era of affordable homes
items.summary: Four new greenfield cities along Rapid Rail Transit System (RRTS) corridors are set to boost real estate affordability in Delhi and its surroundings.  The KMP-Eastern Peripheral Expressway belt is now designated NCR's Ring of Opportunity, ending regulatory uncertainty for peripheral land.  This strategic development, anchored by existing infrastructure like the Delhi-Meerut RRTS and Noida International Airport, positions Sonipat, Bhiwadi, Meerut, and Alwar as independent growth hubs, not just commuter towns.

items.title: Adani Group to enter nuclear power, targets 10 GW by 2035
items.summary: Gautam Adani revealed ambitious expansion plans, including a 10 GW nuclear power venture by 2035 under Adani Atomic Energy. The conglomerate also boosted its thermal power target to 45 GW by FY32 and data centre capacity to 3 GW by 2030. These moves signal a significant push into energy, AI-driven computing, and defence, alongside new partnerships in aircraft manufacturing and hydropower.

items.title: From Rs 25,000 fine to vehicle seizures, Chhattisgarh unveils tough measures to tackle illegal mining
items.summary: The Chhattisgarh government has tightened norms for the release of seized vehicles, machinery and equipment. Now, security deposits ranging from Rs 50,000 to Rs 3 lakh will now be required to be deposited before the concerned court prior to release.

items.title: Natural diamonds are shining again as De Beers-led supply cuts lift prices
items.summary: Natural diamond prices are experiencing a significant rebound, climbing 5-8% year-on-year, driven by reduced supply from major miners and renewed investor interest. Solitaires are increasingly seen as an asset class, with younger generations like Gen Z and millennials leading the demand, viewing them for both aspirational value and investment potential due to their inherent rarity. India plays a crucial role, now accounting for 12% of global diamond demand value.

items.title: India's battery makers say new recycling rules may pull the plug on their business
items.summary: India's dry cell battery giants, including Eveready and Panasonic, are warning of potential shutdowns due to new government recycling rules. Manufacturers claim the mandates, designed for lithium-ion batteries, are financially unviable for zinc-carbon cells, which dominate the market. High compliance costs, estimated to be eight times profitability, and technical recycling limitations threaten the industry's long-standing presence.

items.title: S&P cuts India's FY27 growth forecast to 6.6%, sees inflation at 5.1%
items.summary: India's economic growth is projected to slow to 6.6% in 2026-27 due to energy pressures, a weaker monsoon, and global slowdown, S&P Global Ratings reported. Retail inflation is expected to climb to 5.1% this fiscal year. Despite near-term challenges, GDP growth is anticipated to rebound to 7.2% in 2027-28, with oil prices expected to stabilize.

items.title: MoSPI mulls adding coal assets valuation to national accounts
items.summary: India's statistics ministry is exploring ways to put a price tag on its non-renewable resources, starting with coal. This move aims to provide crucial data for better policymaking and sustainable growth. By valuing coal reserves using a globally recognized method, the government hopes to understand the true economic worth of these vital assets, moving beyond just physical quantities.

items.title: Goyal heads to UK ahead of India-UK trade pact rollout
items.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal's UK visit from June 25-27 precedes the July 15 implementation of the India-UK CETA and Double Contribution Convention. Discussions with UK's Business and Trade Secretary Peter Kyle will focus on regulatory alignment, customs coordination, and operationalizing tariff liberalisation for Indian exports. The talks also aim to boost professional mobility and achieve a bilateral trade target of $120 billion by 2030.

items.title: India, US inch closer to interim trade pact after fresh talks
items.summary: India and the US are nearing an interim trade deal, with both nations reporting significant progress in recent negotiations. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer concluded two days of talks, aiming to finalize the first phase of a bilateral trade agreement. Discussions focused on market access, digital trade, and non-tariff barriers, with both sides committed to a balanced pact delivering tangible benefits.

items.title: Mastercard Move: Who is strategising at Mastercard to eliminate  pain points and make money move globally effortlessly?
items.summary: International payments have traditionally been slow, less transparent, and expensive. With growing expectations of better, seamless payments in India, Mastercard’s Global Head of Transfer Solutions, Pratik Khowala, is radically changing how these payments are carried out. With experience and expertise he is eliminating the pain points.

items.title: DoT notifies new authorisation norms for telcos to shift from licencing regime
items.summary: The government has launched a new authorization-based framework for the telecom sector, replacing the old licensing system. A dedicated portal will facilitate digital implementation of the new Telecom Act. Existing license holders can migrate, and new entities can apply for network or virtual network operator authorizations. Specific rules now govern satellite network operations, requiring Indian gateways and data storage within the country.

items.title: India-China border trade via Lipulekh Pass to resume after 6 yrs
items.summary: Border trade between India and Tibet is set to resume through the Lipulekh pass on June 26, after a six-year halt due to the pandemic. The first batch of 26 Indian traders, including helpers, will enter Chinese territory. A customs office is operational, and preparations are underway for a second batch. Traders anticipate logistical hurdles and will assess goods stored for years before seeking government aid for potential losses.

Factors include a stronger US dollar, rising interest rate expectations, and adjustments in gold forecasts from major banks, resulting in significant losses for both metals this year.

items.title: Manglam Group to invest Rs 350 crore in Sheraton hotel in Jaipur
items.summary: Rajasthan's Manglam Group is investing Rs 350 crore to build a 220-key Sheraton Hotel on the Jaipur-Ajmer Highway, marking their third partnership with Marriott International. This project is part of a larger Rs 1,000 crore hospitality investment plan. The hotel aims to showcase Jaipur's culture and boost its global profile for tourism and business, leveraging its strategic location near key destinations and a major SEZ.

items.summary: The NBFC will strengthen its presence in states including Gujarat, Delhi, Rajasthan, Odisha and Telangana, while expanding deeper into southern and western markets.

items.title: Waterways Leisure Tourism IPO Day 2: Retail portion oversubscribed as Cordelia cruise operator attracts strong interest
items.summary: Waterways Leisure IPO: Waterways Leisure Tourism Limited, which operates Cordelia Cruises, continued to witness robust demand from retail investors, as its initial public offering (IPO) concluded its second day of public bidding, with the retail portion booked 2.34 times.

items.summary: Conclave to bring together hundreds of delegates, policymakers, industry leaders and stakeholders from across the cottonseed, groundnut, edible oil, cake, and meal value chains

items.title: RBI finalises digital banking fraud protection rules; introduces shadow reversal, extends relief to sole proprietors
items.summary: Victims of digital payment fraud losing up to Rs 50,000 can now get up to Rs 25,000 compensation from banks, effective January 1, 2027. The RBI will fund 65% of this, with banks contributing the rest. Sole proprietors are now covered, and cross-border frauds are included. Reporting within five days to the cybercrime portal and bank is crucial for eligibility.

items.title: Zee Business Newsbreak Confirmed: ED flags major irregularities after searches at Rajesh Exports premises
items.summary: ED's official findings validate Zee Business's earlier report; agency flags stock discrepancies, foreign transaction gaps and suspicious share deals during searches at nine locations linked to Rajesh Exports.

items.title: Vedanta sets up real estate arm to unlock value from surplus land
items.summary: Vedanta Ltd is venturing into the real estate sector with the incorporation of its wholly-owned subsidiary, Vedanta Property Platforms Ltd (VPPL). This strategic move aims to unlock value from surplus land and non-core assets, potentially funding expansion in its core metals and energy businesses. VPPL will act as a dedicated platform for real estate ventures and asset-light initiatives.

items.summary: The firm plans to use the funds to strengthen its technology and platform capabilities, and expanding the range of investment products

items.summary: Bandhan Bank has boosted fixed deposit interest rates by up to 20 basis points, with senior citizens now earning a competitive 7.95% on select tenures. This move comes as other banks also adjust their deposit offerings, even with the RBI holding the repo rate steady. The bank aims to attract more retail deposits with these revised, attractive rates.

items.title: CSM Technologies IPO: Issue receives tepid response on Day 01, subscribed 0.26x; GMP signals flat listing
items.summary: CSM Technologies' IPO opened on June 24 but saw low demand, being subscribed 0.26 times on the first day. The  ₹146-crore offering comprises fresh equity shares, with a valuation of approximately  ₹583 crore expected post-issue. Allotment details will be finalized by June 30.

items.summary: More NRIs are approaching India as an investment destination, says Ankur Choudhary, Co-Founder and CEO of Belong

items.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal's UK visit from June 25-27 aims to finalize preparations for the India-UK CETA and DCC, set to launch July 15. Discussions with UK officials will focus on operationalizing these agreements, boosting market access for Indian exports, and easing worker mobility.

items.summary: The weather department further informed that heavy to very heavy rainfall (7-20cm) is likely along the west coast, over northeast India and Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim during the week, with extremely heavy rainfall over Sub-Himalayan West Bengal & Sikkim on 27th & 28th June.

items.title: Why the government is defending E20 fuel after unveiling India’s E100 roadmap
items.summary: Centre’s detailed defence of E20 petrol against viral claims reflects concern over consumer perception, coming after its E100 roadmap, as India pushes beyond blending mandates towards wider ethanol adoption.

items.title: RBI mandates kill switch for AI models at banks, introduces comprehensive model risk framework
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is implementing stringent rules for banks and financial entities using Artificial Intelligence. A new draft framework mandates 'kill switches' for AI models, ensuring immediate shutdown if errors occur. Banks must also ensure human oversight, disclose AI use to customers, and manage risks associated with third-party AI providers. Board-level accountability for AI governance is a key focus, with a risk-based approach to model oversight.

items.title: Nila Spaces and IKEA partner for B2B push, eye growth in India’s emerging ready-to-live homes market
items.summary: IKEA collaborates with Nila Spaces to tap into India's burgeoning ready-to-move-in home market. The partnership aims to meet the demand for functional homes tailored to modern living needs, signifying a shift in real estate toward convenience and personalization for homebuyers.

items.title: Service output index to debut next month with FY25 base
items.summary: India is set to launch the Index of Service Production (ISP) next month, a crucial new monthly indicator mirroring the Industrial Production Index. This initiative aims to precisely track the growth of the vital services sector, which forms the backbone of the Indian economy. The index will initially cover key areas like trade, transport, and finance, with health and education to be included later.

items.title: RBI's Malhotra says rate hike talk is premature, stays in wait-and-watch mode
items.summary: Reserve Bank Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated that a rate hike is premature, emphasizing the central bank's cautious, data-dependent approach. While acknowledging moderating risks from West Asia and falling crude prices, the RBI remains vigilant about potential inflation pressures and second-round effects. The focus is on monitoring fuel price impacts and monsoon progress before making definitive policy decisions.

items.title: Jonnagiri: India’s largest private gold mine set to yield a tonne of gold a year
items.summary: About 12 tonnes of gold lie under Jonnagiri, enough to yield some 400 kg in 2026-27 and pay Andhra Pradesh a 4% royalty

items.title: Subscriber losses push India's TV distribution industry towards consolidation as broadband, IPTV gain ground
items.summary: India's TV distribution is shifting towards broadband and digital entertainment, forcing cable and DTH operators to confront subscriber losses to OTT and free services. This trend is fueling sector consolidation, with companies like ACT Group exiting cable TV to focus on broadband. Operators are repositioning, prioritising IPTV and bundled digital services as key growth drivers amidst declining pay-TV subscriptions.

items.title: RBI expands digital fraud protection, introduces compensation for small-value scam victims
items.summary: The RBI has expanded customer protection rules for digital payment frauds to cover cases where people are tricked into sending money to scammers and introduced a compensation mechanism for small-value frauds. Eligible victims can receive up to 85% of their losses, subject to a cap of ₹25,000. The rules take effect from January 1, 2027.

items.title: US stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq futures rise as tech stocks rebound; Brent slips below $75
items.summary: US stock futures rose modestly after a sell-off in technology stocks, particularly semiconductors. S&amp;P 500 futures increased by 0.2% as investors remain concerned about the valuation and potential returns from recent capital expenditures in AI-related sectors.

items.title: RBI to raise large exposure limit for upper layer NBFC-IFCs to 45% from 35% of eligible capital base
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has boosted lending limits for top infrastructure finance firms, allowing them to extend up to 45% of their capital to connected borrowers, a significant increase from 35%. This move aims to support the crucial infrastructure sector.

items.title: Nifty 50 reclaims 24,000: Five market experts predict the index's short-term outlook
items.summary: The Nifty 50 index has surged past the crucial 24,000 mark, driven by short covering and lower crude oil prices. Market experts share their insights on resistance levels, potential pullbacks, and what investors can expect in the near term.

items.title: Russia and India working on increasing bilateral trade volumes: Kremlin spokesman
items.summary: Russia and India are actively boosting bilateral trade, aiming for a $100 billion target by 2030, up from the current $60 billion. Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov highlighted the "notably privileged partnership" and steady uptrend in economic relations. He praised India as a populous, fast-growing economy with immense potential, underscoring the shared commitment to enhancing cooperation across various international platforms.

items.title: India–Latin America trade set to double to $100 billion by 2030, says Uruguay envoy
items.summary: Speaking at a business and diplomatic conference, Alberto Antonio Guani Amarilla highlighted rising economic cooperation between the two regions and urged deeper engagement across key sectors such as minerals, agro-industries and pharmaceuticals.

items.title: NHAI intensifies drive against illegal highway parking
items.summary: The National Highways Authority of India is intensifying efforts to clear illegal parking and roadside encroachments on national highways, identified as major safety hazards. Focusing on 595 critical spots nationwide, NHAI is collaborating with state authorities to remove these obstructions. Additionally, the authority is ensuring all traffic management systems are operational and enhancing emergency response with more ambulances and patrols for safer commutes.

items.title: COMA seeks regulation of coconut trade to curb illegal transactions
items.summary: Unchecked inflow and outflow of coconuts and coconut products through informal channels are adversely affecting Kerala’s coconut industry, says COMA

items.title: Motilal Oswal initiates coverage on 8 textile stocks, including Arvind and Gokaldas Exports; sees up to 39% upside
items.summary: Motilal Oswal has started coverage on eight textile firms, predicting growth due to global demand recovery and capacity expansions. They recommend 'buy' for Gokaldas Exports, Arvind, Pearl Global, Indo Count, and Welspun Living.&nbsp;

items.title: Finance ministry, NFRA officials brief Par panel on corporate laws amendment bill
items.summary: Parliament's joint committee received briefings on the Corporate Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2026, from Finance Ministry officials and the National Financial Reporting Authority. This proposed legislation aims to simplify business operations by decriminalizing minor procedural errors and modernising corporate governance.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex jumps 790 points, Nifty reclaims 24,000 as banking and IT stocks power rally
items.summary: Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Highlights: Indian benchmark indices staged a strong recovery on Wednesday, with Sensex rising nearly 800 points and Nifty reclaiming the 24,000 mark. Gains in banking and IT stocks, along with easing crude oil prices, lifted investor sentiment.

items.title: Western Railway upgrades key routes for faster trains, eyes 160 kmph on Mumbai-Delhi corridor
items.summary: Western Railway is significantly boosting train speeds, with several routes set to reach 130 kmph and the Mumbai-Delhi corridor aiming for 160 kmph. This upgrade aims to slash travel times and enhance efficiency. Additionally, plans are in motion to introduce Vande Bharat sleeper trains and expand platform capacity at Mumbai Central to accommodate longer trains, while Garib Nagar in Bandra will see passenger amenity development.

items.title: India to release first-ever monthly index tracking services sector output from July 14
items.summary: India is launching a new Index of Services Production (ISP) to track the services sector's growth, mirroring the Index of Industrial Production. This crucial macroeconomic indicator, with 2024-25 as the base year, will be released monthly with a 60-day lag, starting with April 2026 data on July 14. The ISP aims to provide high-frequency insights into the dominant services sector's performance, complementing existing economic data.

items.title: India ranks third globally in AI readiness, remains among least concentrated equity markets: JPMorgan
items.summary: JPMorgan report highlights India’s growing AI readiness, ranking it third globally after the US and China. While the US leads in AI infrastructure and innovation, China is gaining ground with cost-efficient models.

items.title: Bank of India eyes $3.5 billion foreign currency inflows on RBI's dollar push
items.summary: Bank of India aims to raise approximately $3.5 billion through foreign currency deposits and overseas borrowings, leveraging recent RBI measures. This strategic move, including $1 billion from FCNR(B) deposits and $1.5 billion via concessional swap facilities, is expected to reduce borrowing costs by 50-60 basis points.

items.title: Amid ‘extraordinary scrutiny’, Adani Group invested ₹1.5 lakh crore in FY26: Gautam Adani
items.summary: At the company’s AGM, Adani said the group continued to expand across energy, transport, logistics, airports, digital infrastructure and industrial manufacturing despite global uncertainties and heightened scrutiny.

items.title: FRAI urges govt to intensify crackdown on counterfeit products, illicit trade
items.summary: Retailers are urging the government to intensify efforts against illegal trade and fake products, citing risks to consumer safety and the livelihoods of small businesses. The Federation of Retailers Association of India (FRAI) has called for stricter enforcement, tougher penalties, and greater accountability from online marketplaces. They highlighted a significant rise in counterfeit goods across various sectors, impacting fair competition and consumer trust.

items.summary: Holiday home brand SaffronStays has secured $3.5 million in a funding round led by Infinity Ventures. This capital will fuel expansion into new leisure destinations, enhance technology, and improve guest experiences. The company, profitable for four years, aims to build five regional business units generating ₹100 crore annually, emphasizing profitability and customer satisfaction in India's growing managed holiday-home market.

items.title: Amazon scales rapid delivery ‘Now’ to 300+ cities; launches ‘Sammaan’ program for delivery partner welfare
items.summary: Things needed quickly, like groceries, shampoo, baby products, get picked and delivered just minutes after being ordered, Amazon CEO Andy Jassy said, adding that “customers are loving it...”&nbsp;

items.summary: Banks are constrained for credit only when they do not have sufficient capital and we (banks) have sufficient capital, the RBI Governor said

items.title: India, US conclude two-day trade talks
items.summary: India and the United States concluded ministerial talks on a proposed trade agreement, aiming to finalize an initial deal before Washington's temporary tariffs expire. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer met in New Delhi to review progress and explore ways to strengthen economic ties. Both sides expressed appreciation for the constructive discussions, highlighting a forward-looking approach to deepening the bilateral partnership.

items.title: BHIM Payments App transaction volumes triple in year: Official
items.summary: BHIM Payments App witnessed a remarkable surge, with transaction volumes tripling between June 2025 and May 2026, reaching 244 million monthly. In Telangana, this digital payment adoption is evident, with groceries, food outlets, and quick commerce leading merchant transactions. This growth highlights the increasing reliance on digital methods for daily expenses, with the app's multi-language support and offline capabilities fostering wider accessibility.

items.title: Indiamart vs OpenAI case: India's IT intermediary framework must adapt to the age of AI
items.summary: The country’s framework of IT rules has been overtaken by tech evolution. Today, as judicial observations show, AI chatbots pose a safe-harbour dilemma. Legal liability is not easy to pin on platforms that generate content but only in response to user prompts.

items.title: India, US move closer to interim trade deal after Goyal-Greer talks
items.summary: India and the United States have made significant headway in their Bilateral Trade Agreement negotiations, with both nations expressing strong commitment to an interim deal. Discussions focused on market access, digital trade, and supply chains, aiming for a pact that benefits businesses and consumers. This push to finalize an initial agreement before upcoming US tariff expirations marks a crucial step in deepening economic ties.

items.title: Global giants backing India’s shipbuilding boom, says shipping secretary
items.summary: Driven by a  ₹70,000-crore policy package, domestic yards have secured huge export orders while the government backs five massive manufacturing clusters to scale up capacity, Vijay Kumar, secretary at the shipping ministry, told Mint in an exclusive interview.

items.title: Adani Group to outsource non-core activities or move to GCCs: Gautam Adani
items.summary: Group taking three fundamental steps to execute vision over next decade, building 3 GW data centre platform, 10 GW nuclear energy capacity

items.title: Adani Ports bags 10-year marine services contract for Argentina's first LNG export project
items.summary: Under the agreement, the consortium will provide end-to-end marine services, including tugboat operations for LNG carriers, offshore logistics and supply support, and crew transfer services

items.title: Sublime enters travel retail with first airport store at Bengaluru Airport Terminal 2
items.summary: Sublime has opened its first airport store at Bengaluru Airport's Terminal 2, offering luxury teas, dry fruits, spices, honey, crockery and curated gifting options.

items.title: Adani Energy to acquire smart metering firm IntelliSmart for ₹3,050 crore
items.summary: The proposed acquisition will strengthen AESL’s position as India’s largest smart metering platform with over 4.7 crore smart meters

items.title: MoSPI releases discussion paper to identify apt method to compile coal monetary asset accounts
items.summary: India's Ministry of Statistics has released a discussion paper on valuing coal assets monetarily, aligning with UN environmental accounting standards. This initiative aims to provide crucial data for policymaking and sustainable development by assessing resource value and depletion costs. The paper also presents physical coal accounts, highlighting India's significant production and its role in the nation's energy security and industrial growth. Feedback is now invited.

items.title: Adani opens Mundra Airport for scheduled commercial flights to 4 cities
items.summary: With this, Mundra has become the 9th airport of the Adani Group, and this location is set to become a fully integrated, multi-modal logistics and business hub

items.title: Today’s Fed chief is said to be an admirer of Greenspan (1926-2026) but he mustn’t take it too far
items.summary: Kevin Warsh, the new chair of the US Federal Reserve may admire the ‘maestro,’ but he must not misread Greenspan’s legacy. Warsh must remind himself of what went wrong under the late former Fed chief—and how times have changed for monetary policy.

items.title: India's upstart consumer brands are growing three times faster than the market
items.summary: New-age Indian brands are rapidly outperforming established FMCG companies, growing over three times faster and reshaping various consumer categories. These "insurgent" brands, fueled by digital and quick commerce, are capturing market share by addressing unmet consumer needs with rapid innovation. While they still hold a small market percentage, their accelerated growth poses a significant challenge to larger players, forcing them to adapt to evolving consumer behavior and new market dynamics.

items.title: India-Latin America trade to reach $100 billion by 2030 from $50 billion currently: Uruguay Ambassador
items.summary: Amarilla, Ambassador of Uruguay to India and Coordinator of the Latin American and Caribbean Group (GRULAC), told ANI on Wednesday, highlighting the growing economic partnership between the two regions.

items.title: Noel exiting Trent: The quiet Tata who built a loud success story
items.summary: Noel Tata reportedly told shareholders at Trent's annual general meeting that it would be his last as chairman, signalling the close of a transformative era. Under his leadership, Trent evolved from a modest retailer into one of India's most valuable consumer businesses, with Westside and Zudio driving growth as revenue crossed Rs 20,000 crore and profits surged.

items.title: India's GIC Re plans to reduce share of overseas property risk as climate losses rise
items.summary: Indian reinsurer GIC Re is strategically shifting its overseas business focus. Amidst escalating climate-related losses globally, the company plans to reduce its exposure to property and catastrophe risks abroad. Instead, GIC Re aims to bolster its presence in casualty and specialty insurance lines, seeking a more balanced international portfolio over the next three to five years.

items.title: VOC Port emerges as model for sustainable maritime development
items.summary: Leading the charge in India's sustainable maritime evolution, the V.O. Chidambaranar Port Authority (VOCPA) has made impressive strides in cutting carbon emissions and almost entirely covering its energy requirements with renewable resources. Union Minister Sarbananda Sonowal showcased these accomplishments during the launch of a new Kendriya Vidyalaya, highlighted an IIM Calcutta case study detailing the port's transition to greener practices, and introduced a new mobile app powered by generative AI.

items.title: Hyundai Motor's South Korean union approves strike action over stalled wage talks
items.summary: Hyundai Motor's South Korean union members have voted to authorize a strike, signaling potential production halts. This action stems from unresolved wage talks, with the union demanding a significant base pay increase and a substantial performance bonus. They are also seeking assurances on employment and working conditions concerning AI integration, mirroring recent profit-sharing moves by other major tech firms.

items.title: India's FY27 growth likely to slow down to 6.6% on energy stress, sub-par monsoon: S&P
items.summary: India's economic growth is projected to slow to 6.6% in the current fiscal year, according to S&P Global Ratings. This slowdown is attributed to energy market pressures, a potentially weak monsoon, and a global economic deceleration. Higher energy and fertilizer costs are expected to fuel inflation, potentially leading to a policy rate hike in the latter half of the fiscal year.

items.title: Cummins India bets on local demand as Middle East conflict clouds exports
items.summary: Cummins India anticipates robust domestic demand to fuel its fiscal 2027 growth, despite ongoing Middle East geopolitical tensions impacting exports.
